title: Fishhawk operator role overlay v0 (.fishhawk/operator.yaml)
description: Schema for the per-repo operator role overlay (ADR-040 D1). The overlay is thin by design: it may only select knob presets, set local conventions (merge-ritual specifics, escalation contacts/channels), and point at the repo's work-management config. It cannot define procedure — procedure added per-repo is definitionally a product gap; file an issue. The procedure fields below are declared with always-failing subschemas so a violation names the thinness rule.

role.description: Optional role identity, for symmetry with the base spec.

spec_version.description: The base role spec version this overlay targets. Single-value enum per the docs/spec versioning rules.

knob_presets.description: Named preset selection (snake_case key, preset name value). Reserves the key shape only; preset values and their evaluation against the workflow spec's operator_agent knobs are #1026's scope and may tighten this additively within v0.

conventions.description: Local conventions merged into the base spec's conventions section: merge-ritual specifics, escalation contacts/channels. Prose values only — a convention that reads like a procedure step belongs in the product.

work_management.description: Opaque pointer to the repo's work-management config (#1005/#1012). Resolution is the consumer's concern; this schema only reserves the key.

not.$comment: thinness rule (ADR-040 D1): overlays cannot define procedure — per-repo procedure is a product gap; file an issue
thinness rule (ADR-040 D1): overlays cannot define procedure — per-repo procedure is a product gap; file an issue
thinness rule (ADR-040 D1): overlays cannot define procedure — per-repo procedure is a product gap; file an issue (escalation contacts/channels go under conventions)
thinness rule (ADR-040 D1): overlays cannot define procedure — per-repo procedure is a product gap; file an issue
